mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or 20-day forward returns are positive in over 65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack triggers immediate profit margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered observations despite the threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real GDP and housing starts signal a broad demand contraction that depresses corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, industrial production growth above trend indicates capacity constraints that force firms to raise prices, boosting equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of late-cycle periods when INDPRO exceeds its 5-year moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% triggers immediate liquidity withdrawal from risk assets, generating negative S&P 500 returns within a one-week trading window.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 58% of observations when the rate threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates indicates labor hoarding during economic stress, which suppresses corporate profitability and yields negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no predictive power for 60-day returns, with an out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing market correction regime, declining retail sales directly reduce consumer discretionary spending forecasts, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 62% of housing correction periods despite declining RSAFS values.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production levels reflect stronger aggregate output and corporate revenue generation, producing positive S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of periods when INDPRO registers positive month-over-month growth.
